precipitation-blues.notes: Gaussian blur sigma=3 before colormapping. Current QML uses pink/magenta — MUST be replaced. Consider lowering domain ceiling to 50mm or gamma<1 to push more pixels into mid-blues.

soil-moisture-browns.reference: No direct video reference — unique to our narrative
soil-moisture-browns.notes: Brown→green→blue encodes dry→moist→saturated. Current QGIS render uses blues; earth tones would better serve the 'sponge fills' chapter. Test both in Phase 2.

sst-diverging.reference: Standard RdBu diverging — warm Atlantic anomaly visually dominant
sst-diverging.notes: QGIS render confirmed: warm orange-red covers most Atlantic during Jan 2026, clearly communicating anomalous heat reservoir. White midpoint at 0°C.

ivt-sequential.reference: Atmospheric river corridor — vivid red-green-yellow in QGIS render
ivt-sequential.notes: QGIS render shows vivid green-red multicolor. For web, switch to purple→white sequential for consistency with dark ocean basemap. The AR core at 40°N directly targets Portugal.

satellite-ir.reference: contact-satellite-motion.png — our STRONGEST layer, 80-90% of reference
satellite-ir.notes: Cold cloud tops = bright white, warm surface = dark. High contrast achieved. Minor tweak: darken warm end slightly for more cloud/ocean separation.

flood-extent.reference: wx-audit/24-flood-extent-fixed.png — clean blue on dark navy
flood-extent.notes: Ghost pulse in Ch.0 at 3% opacity. Full 55% in Ch.1. Ch.7 overlaid with burn scars — blue/amber contrast passes deuteranopia.

flood-depth.reference: No video reference — aerial hybrid basemap specific
flood-depth.notes: Blue gradient over satellite imagery. QGIS test with Esri World Imagery at Salvaterra confirmed: light blue shallow, dark blue medium, deep red extreme. Graduated alpha (0.6→0.9) keeps terrain visible through shallow flood.

ipma-warnings.notes: TESTED as gold district outlines over dark synoptic. Faint fill + bold outline keeps warning zones visible without obscuring weather data. Red/orange distinction problematic for deuteranopia — add pattern fills or labels as Phase 2 accessibility measure.

burn-scars.reference: No video reference — unique to our fire→flood narrative
burn-scars.notes: 3,147 wildfire polygons in amber on ultra-dark navy. Ch.7 reveal: blue flood + amber fire = immediately legible. Passes deuteranopia (blue→purple, amber→olive-yellow, still distinct).

discharge-ratio.notes: Blue (normal) → white (moderate) → red (extreme flood). Applied as circle radius + color on 11 discharge stations. Station positions over watershed basins with river network.

precip-7day.notes: 7-day rolling window shows antecedent moisture buildup. Peak 324mm on Feb 7. Uses deeper blues than daily precip to differentiate visually. Reference uses green ramp — we chose blues for consistency with project palette.

mslp-contour.reference: contact-mslp-animation.png — white/cream contours creating bullseye depth
mslp-contour.notes: CRITICAL FIX from impact gauge: 0.8px gray → 1.5px white. QGIS test confirmed dramatic improvement. Bullseye depth effect around lows now visible. L/H markers at pressure centers in red/blue.
